category_title: Branching tree with three independent rotary branches
prompt: An under-slung three-branch rotary frame. A top bridge supports three hanging branch arms on separate brackets, making the fixed support read differently from the moving arms used in the other variants. The hanging arms are independent revolute joints on their own supports.
label: Branching tree with three independent rotary branches One Each 1

category_title: Branching tree with two independent rotary branches
prompt: A hub-and-spoke two-branch motion rig. A vertical mast carries two short spoke arms on discrete hub blocks, and each hub block is visibly larger than the arm it supports. The spoke arms are independent revolute joints about supported hub axes.
label: Branching tree with two independent rotary branches One Each 1

prompt: A compact bench cantilever arm. A short column base carries two rigid links and a compact wrist face, with the whole arm clearly overhanging one side of the base. The mechanism is a serial three-revolute arm.
label: Cantilever articulated arm One Each 1

prompt: An under-slung coaxial stage stack. A top support and center shaft carry multiple concentric stages below, giving the stack a hanging silhouette. The concentric stages rotate as separate revolute joints about one shared axis.
label: Coaxial rotary stack One Each 1

prompt: An under-slung dual finger rig. A top support carries a common palm block and two finger chains hanging beneath it, giving the pair a different silhouette while keeping the rigid-link structure obvious. Each hanging finger uses three revolute joints and remains mechanically independent.
label: Dual independent finger chains One Each 1

prompt: A narrow service lift on twin rails. Two fixed uprights support a compact moving saddle with a square work face and clear overlap on both guides. The saddle is a single vertical prismatic stage.
label: Dual-column lift carriage One Each 1

prompt: A compact service gantry. A fixed lower frame supports a moving bridge and a short cross-slide whose support hardware remains visibly larger than the tool face it carries. The moving bridge and cross-slide are orthogonal prismatic joints.
label: Dual-rail gantry axis One Each 1

prompt: A fork-root digit assembly. A fixed fork captures the proximal phalanx, followed by two smaller phalanx links and a rounded tip pad. The proximal, middle, and distal links are connected by three revolute joints.
label: Finger-like phalanx chain One Each 1

prompt: A slim service folding arm chain. A narrow grounded spine supports a sequence of simple rigid links with compact joint cheeks and a small end pad at the end. The mechanism uses three serial revolute joints about parallel axes.
label: Folding arm chain One Each 1

prompt: A slim service four-joint revolute chain. A narrow grounded spine supports a sequence of simple rigid links with compact joint cheeks and a compact end tab at the end. The mechanism uses four serial revolute joints about parallel axes.
label: Four-joint revolute chain One Each 1

prompt: A vertical-input transfer gear assembly. A fixed base supports a vertical input shaft that passes through a right-angle stage into a horizontal reduction stage. The vertical and horizontal shafts are separate revolute joints carried by the grounded supports.
label: Gear Assemblies One Each 1

prompt: A production-style exposed joystick gimbal. A grounded base block supports a nested gimbal set and a straight control stick with the support structure left open and legible. The gimbal provides two orthogonal revolute joints about intersecting axes.
label: Joystick mechanism One Each 1

prompt: A bridge-mounted lever chain. A fixed bridge support carries a root clevis and a sequence of links whose section sizes taper toward the distal small end tab. The chain has three revolute joints in series.
label: Lever chain One Each 1

category_title: Linear carriage with independent rotary end-effector
prompt: A compact service shuttle with spindle tip. A grounded body supports a short carriage, and the carriage carries a visibly smaller spindle cartridge at one end. The carriage is one prismatic joint and the spindle is one revolute joint.
label: Linear carriage with independent rotary end-effector One Each 1

prompt: A narrow service fork carriage. A compact fixed mast supports a smaller moving platen with a load guard and paired forks. The moving platen is one vertical prismatic joint on the mast.
label: Mast-mounted fork carriage One Each 1

prompt: A compact console monitor arm without the monitor. A grounded base bracket supports two short articulated links and a small head frame that can fold close to the base. Two revolute joints fold the arm, and the head adds separate swivel and tilt revolute joints.
label: Monitor mount One Each 1

prompt: A slim service multi-segment fold-out arm. A narrow grounded spine supports a sequence of simple rigid links with compact joint cheeks and a small platform bracket at the end. The mechanism uses four serial revolute joints about parallel axes.
label: Multi-segment fold-out arm One Each 1

prompt: An under-slung offset rotary assembly. A top bridge support carries a lower rotary stage and a smaller upper stage that hangs from the bridge on a separate axis. The two stages are independent revolute joints on parallel supported axes.
label: Offset-axis rotary stack One Each 1

prompt: A bridge-backed parallel gripper. A rear frame supports a short center housing and two outward guideways leading to mirrored jaw carriages. The jaw carriages are separate prismatic joints along the grip axis.
label: Opposed twin-slide gripper One Each 1

prompt: A compact service XY axis. A fixed lower guide supports a long carriage, and the carriage supports a shorter cross-slide with a square output face. The lower and upper stages are prismatic joints on crossed horizontal directions.
label: Orthogonal XY stage One Each 1

prompt: A compact service XZ stage. A fixed guide body supports a broad lower carriage and a slimmer vertical guide rising from it. The lower carriage and upper guide are orthogonal prismatic joints.
label: Orthogonal XZ stage One Each 1

prompt: A compact service YZ stage. A fixed back support carries a short lateral slide and a visibly smaller hanging Z carriage. The lateral and vertical stages are orthogonal prismatic joints.
label: Orthogonal YZ stage One Each 1

prompt: A tower-mounted pitch-roll unit. A short pedestal supports a compact two-axis head whose outer member is visibly larger than the inner output cradle. The outer member rolls on a revolute joint and the inner cradle pitches on a perpendicular revolute joint.
label: Pitch-roll module One Each 1

prompt: A compact service portal with lift stage. A grounded frame supports a broad horizontal carriage and a smaller vertical stage nested into its face. The horizontal carriage is one prismatic joint and the nested vertical stage is a second prismatic joint.
label: Portal gantry with vertical slide One Each 1

prompt: A compact service slide-hinge module. A broad grounded body supports one short carriage and one small hinged tab. The short carriage is a prismatic joint and the tab is a carried revolute joint.
label: Prismatic-revolute chain One Each 1

prompt: A compact service slide-link-slide module. A broad grounded body supports one carriage, one pivot frame, and one short terminal slider with plain hard edges. The carriage is prismatic, the pivot frame is revolute, and the terminal slider is prismatic.
label: Prismatic-revolute-prismatic chain One Each 1

prompt: A fork-backed slide with articulated tip. A rear support frame carries a prismatic carriage and a two-link arm mounted on the carriage. The carriage is a prismatic joint and the arm uses two serial revolute joints.
label: Prismatic-revolute-revolute chain One Each 1

prompt: A fork-backed plunger chain. A rear support frame carries a guide sleeve and a short lever in front of it, with the plunger tip driving the lever root. The sleeve and plunger are one prismatic joint, and the lever is one revolute joint.
label: Push-pull plunger chain One Each 1

prompt: A compact service radial arm. A broad grounded base supports one sweeping arm and one small sliding head on the arm. The sweeping arm is a revolute joint and the sliding head is a prismatic joint.
label: Radial arm with sliding carriage One Each 1

prompt: A compact service arm with linear nose. A broad grounded base supports one rigid link and one tip slider that stays clearly smaller than the link. The mechanism uses one revolute joint followed by one prismatic joint.
label: Revolute-prismatic chain One Each 1

prompt: A compact service hinge-slider-wrist module. A broad grounded base supports one pivot frame, one extension stage, and one small rotating tip member. The pivot frame is revolute, the extension is prismatic, and the tip member is revolute.
label: Revolute-prismatic-revolute chain One Each 1

prompt: A fork-backed arm with telescoping nose. A rear support captures the first arm link, followed by a second link and a short output extension. The two arm links articulate through revolute joints, and the extension is a prismatic joint.
label: Revolute-revolute-prismatic chain One Each 1

prompt: A compact service pan-and-lift unit. A broad grounded body supports a lower rotary stage and a smaller vertical carriage above it. The lower stage rotates on a revolute joint and the upper carriage slides on a vertical prismatic joint.
label: Rotary base with vertical slide One Each 1

prompt: A compact service trunnion table. A broad grounded body supports a lower rotary base and a smaller upper work face held in side cheeks. The lower base rotates on a revolute joint and the upper work face tilts on a trunnion revolute joint.
label: Rotary table with tilting trunnion One Each 1

prompt: A fork-root elbow module. A fixed fork captures the upper arm root, and the upper arm carries the forearm to a plain end plate. The root fork supports the shoulder revolute and the upper arm supports the elbow revolute.
label: Serial elbow arm One Each 1

prompt: A fork-root three-joint arm. A grounded fork captures the first arm link, followed by a forearm and a short wrist flange. The arm has shoulder, elbow, and wrist revolute joints in sequence.
label: Shoulder-elbow-wrist arm One Each 1

prompt: An under-slung rotary shaft unit. A top bracket supports a short shaft between two hanging bearings so the grounded support remains visually distinct from the spinning member. The shaft rotates as one continuous revolute joint about the supported centerline.
label: Single continuous rotary shaft One Each 1

prompt: An under-slung pitch-axis trunnion module. A top support bracket carries the moving member below it, creating a different silhouette while keeping the same single-axis kinematics. One revolute joint tilts the carried face about a horizontal trunnion axis supported by side cheeks.
label: Single pitch axis module One Each 1

prompt: A bridge-backed slide unit. A grounded back frame supports a front-running carriage with a plain mounting face and visible stop features. The carriage translates through one prismatic joint on the guide path.
label: Single prismatic slider One Each 1

prompt: A boxed cover hinge. A fixed bracket leaf carries short knuckle segments and a moving cover leaf with a simple edge flange. The cover leaf rotates as one revolute joint about the supported barrel axis.
label: Single revolute hinge One Each 1

prompt: An under-slung roll-axis spindle module. A top support bracket carries the moving member below it, creating a different silhouette while keeping the same single-axis kinematics. One revolute joint spins the output flange about its own longitudinal shaft axis between fixed supports.
label: Single roll axis module One Each 1

prompt: An under-slung yaw-axis rotary module. A top support bracket carries the moving member below it, creating a different silhouette while keeping the same single-axis kinematics. One revolute joint turns the output face about the vertical support axis.
label: Single yaw axis module One Each 1

prompt: A compact service telescoping boom. A broad grounded root body supports an outer tube and several inner tubes with clear overlap left between every stage. The inner tubes extend through serial prismatic joints.
label: Telescoping boom One Each 1

prompt: A fork-backed telescoping mast assembly. A rear support captures the mast root and carries nested upright stages ending in a small rotary faceplate. The mast uses serial vertical prismatic joints and the faceplate is a revolute joint at the top.
label: Telescoping mast with pan head One Each 1

prompt: A compact service three-stage axis. A broad grounded body supports three carried sliders with plain rectangular faces and visible overlap. The stage stack is three serial prismatic joints.
label: Three-joint prismatic chain One Each 1

prompt: A bridge-mounted three-joint revolute chain. A fixed bridge support carries a root clevis and a sequence of links whose section sizes taper toward the distal compact end tab. The chain has three revolute joints in series.
label: Three-joint revolute chain One Each 1

prompt: A compact service three-stage slide. A broad grounded body supports two nested moving stages with plain rectangular front faces. The moving stages are serial prismatic joints.
label: Three-stage telescoping slide One Each 1

prompt: A long-reach service TV wall mount without the panel. A grounded wall bracket carries two arm links and a compact output frame, with the links distinctly longer than the head support. The two links fold on revolute joints, and the head adds one swivel and one tilt revolute joint.
label: TV wall mount One Each 1

prompt: A compact service two-stage axis. A broad grounded body supports one main carriage and one smaller terminal carriage. The two stages are serial prismatic joints.
label: Two-joint prismatic chain One Each 1

prompt: A bridge-mounted two-joint revolute chain. A fixed bridge support carries a root clevis and a sequence of links whose section sizes taper toward the distal compact end tab. The chain has two revolute joints in series.
label: Two-joint revolute chain One Each 1

prompt: A compact service telescoping slide. A broad grounded body supports a simple nested runner with clear remaining overlap when extended. The runner is a prismatic joint along the slide axis.
label: Two-stage telescoping slide One Each 1

prompt: A fork-backed louver array. A rear support frame carries a stack of parallel slats whose supports are visible at both ends. The slats articulate as independent revolute joints.
label: Vane array with independent pivots One Each 1

prompt: A fork-backed slide-and-wrist unit. A rear support frame carries a vertical slide carriage with a short hinged face at the output. The slide carriage is prismatic and the face is a revolute joint.
label: Vertical slide with wrist hinge One Each 1

prompt: A compact service XYZ stage. A broad grounded base supports a long lower carriage, a shorter cross-slide, and a slim vertical ram. The lower, middle, and upper members are three prismatic joints.
label: XYZ Cartesian stage One Each 1

prompt: A tower-mounted yaw-pitch module. A short pedestal supports a yaw base and a carried pitch yoke, creating a rooted upright silhouette. The base yaws on a vertical revolute joint and the yoke pitches on a horizontal revolute joint.
label: Yaw-pitch module One Each 1

prompt: A saddle-body multi-axis wrist. A grounded housing supports a yaw stage, a pitch support, and a compact roll cartridge, tapering in size toward the output. The three motions are serial revolute joints about vertical, crosswise horizontal, and longitudinal axes.
label: Yaw-pitch-roll wrist One Each 1
